--- title: "Design & Analysis of Field Experiments using R" author: "Muhammad Yaseen" date: 2020-03-06 format: html: page-layout: full comments: false slug: 2020-03-06-UAF categories: - R - Statistics - Research --- ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ::: ## Introduction >**R** is a free, open-source programming language and software environment for statistical computing, bioinformatics, visualization and general computing. **R** provides a wide variety of statistical and graphical techniques, and is highly extensible. The latest version of **R** can be obtained from [https://cran.r-project.org/bin/windows/base/](https://cran.r-project.org/bin/windows/base/).

  ## Regression Analysis > * Quantifying dependency of a normal response on quantitative explanatory variable(s)   ::: {#fig-PopRegFn} ![](./fig/PopRegFn.webp) Population Regression Function :::   ## Simple Linear Regression > * Quantifying dependency of a normal response on a quantitative explanatory variable   ### Example Fertilizer (Kg/acre) | Production (Bushels/acre) ------------| ------------- 100 | 70 200 | 70 400 | 80 500 | 100 ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} # Fertilizer (Kg/acre) # Production (Bushels/acre) Fertilizer <- c(100, 200, 400, 500) Production <- c( 70, 70, 80, 100) df1 <- data.frame(Fertilizer, Production) df1 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Fertilizer Production 1 100 70 2 200 70 3 400 80 4 500 100 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} library(ggplot2) # if (!require("ggplot2")) install.packages("ggplot2") p1 <- ggplot(data = df1, mapping = aes(x = Fertilizer, y = Production)) + geom_point() + labs(x = "Fertilizer", y = "Production") + theme_bw() print(p1) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](2020-03-06_UAF_files/figure-html/reg.p1-1.svg){fig-align='center' width=672} ::: ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} fm1 <- lm(formula = Production ~ Fertilizer, data = df1) summary(fm1) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Call: lm(formula = Production ~ Fertilizer, data = df1) Residuals: 1 2 3 4 4 -3 -7 6 Coefficients: Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|) (Intercept) 59.00000 7.95299 7.419 0.0177 * Fertilizer 0.07000 0.02345 2.985 0.0963 . --- Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 Residual standard error: 7.416 on 2 degrees of freedom Multiple R-squared: 0.8167, Adjusted R-squared: 0.725 F-statistic: 8.909 on 1 and 2 DF, p-value: 0.0963 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} anova(fm1) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Analysis of Variance Table Response: Production Df Sum Sq Mean Sq F value Pr(>F) Fertilizer 1 490 490 8.9091 0.0963 . Residuals 2 110 55 --- Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 ``` ::: :::   ### Example Fertilizer | Yield ------------| ------------- 0.3 | 10 0.6 | 15 0.9 | 30 1.2 | 35 1.5 | 25 1.8 | 30 2.1 | 50 2.4 | 45 ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} Fert <- c(0.3, 0.6, 0.9, 1.2, 1.5, 1.8, 2.1, 2.4) Yield <- c(10, 15, 30, 35, 25, 30, 50, 45) df2 <- data.frame(Fert, Yield) df2 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Fert Yield 1 0.3 10 2 0.6 15 3 0.9 30 4 1.2 35 5 1.5 25 6 1.8 30 7 2.1 50 8 2.4 45 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} library(ggplot2) # if (!require("ggplot2")) install.packages("ggplot2") p2 <- ggplot(data = df2, mapping = aes(x = Fert, y = Yield)) + geom_point() + labs(x = "Fertilizer", y = "Yield") + theme_bw() print(p2) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](2020-03-06_UAF_files/figure-html/reg.p2-1.svg){fig-align='center' width=672} ::: ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} fm2 <- lm(formula = Yield ~ Fert, data = df2) summary(fm2) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Call: lm(formula = Yield ~ Fert, data = df2) Residuals: Min 1Q Median 3Q Max -7.441 -4.018 -2.441 7.351 7.798 Coefficients: Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|) (Intercept) 8.036 5.504 1.460 0.1946 Fert 16.270 3.633 4.478 0.0042 ** --- Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 Residual standard error: 7.064 on 6 degrees of freedom Multiple R-squared: 0.7697, Adjusted R-squared: 0.7313 F-statistic: 20.05 on 1 and 6 DF, p-value: 0.004202 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} anova(fm2) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Analysis of Variance Table Response: Yield Df Sum Sq Mean Sq F value Pr(>F) Fert 1 1000.6 1000.6 20.052 0.004202 ** Residuals 6 299.4 49.9 --- Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 ``` ::: :::   ### Example Weekly Income (\$) | Weekly Expenditures (\$) ------------| ------------- 80 | 70 100 | 65 120 | 90 140 | 95 160 | 110 180 | 115 200 | 120 220 | 140 240 | 155 260 | 150 ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} # Weekly Income ($) of a Family # Weekly Expenditures ($) of a Family Income <- seq(from = 80, to = 260, by = 20) Expenditures <- c(70, 65, 90, 95, 110, 115, 120, 140, 155, 150) df3 <- data.frame(Income, Expenditures) df3 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Income Expenditures 1 80 70 2 100 65 3 120 90 4 140 95 5 160 110 6 180 115 7 200 120 8 220 140 9 240 155 10 260 150 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} library(ggplot2) # if (!require("ggplot2")) install.packages("ggplot2") p3 <- ggplot(data = df3, mapping = aes(x = Income, y = Expenditures)) + geom_point() + scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::dollar) + scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::dollar) + labs(x = "Weekly income, $", y = "Weekly consumption expenditures, $") + theme_bw() print(p3) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](2020-03-06_UAF_files/figure-html/reg.p3-1.svg){fig-align='center' width=672} ::: ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} fm3 <- lm(formula = Expenditures ~ Income, data = df3) summary(fm3) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Call: lm(formula = Expenditures ~ Income, data = df3) Residuals: Min 1Q Median 3Q Max -10.364 -4.977 1.409 4.364 8.364 Coefficients: Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|) (Intercept) 24.45455 6.41382 3.813 0.00514 ** Income 0.50909 0.03574 14.243 5.75e-07 *** --- Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 Residual standard error: 6.493 on 8 degrees of freedom Multiple R-squared: 0.9621, Adjusted R-squared: 0.9573 F-statistic: 202.9 on 1 and 8 DF, p-value: 5.753e-07 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} anova(fm3) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Analysis of Variance Table Response: Expenditures Df Sum Sq Mean Sq F value Pr(>F) Income 1 8552.7 8552.7 202.87 5.753e-07 *** Residuals 8 337.3 42.2 --- Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 ``` ::: :::   ## Multiple Linear Regression > * Quantifying dependency of a normal response on two or more quantitative explanatory variables   ### Example Fertilizer (Kg) | Rainfall (mm) | Yield (Kg) ------------| -------------| ------------- 100 | 10 | 40 200 | 20 | 50 300 | 10 | 50 400 | 30 | 70 500 | 20 | 65 600 | 20 | 65 700 | 30 | 80 ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} # Fertilizer (Kg) # Rainfall (mm) # Yield (Kg) Fertilizer <- seq(from = 100, to = 700, by = 100) Rainfall <- c(10, 20, 10, 30, 20, 20, 30) Yield <- c(40, 50, 50, 70, 65, 65, 80) df4 <- data.frame(Fertilizer, Rainfall, Yield) df4 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Fertilizer Rainfall Yield 1 100 10 40 2 200 20 50 3 300 10 50 4 400 30 70 5 500 20 65 6 600 20 65 7 700 30 80 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} library(car) # if (!require("car")) install.packages("car") car::scatter3d(formula = Yield ~ Fertilizer + Rainfall, data = df4) ``` ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} fm4 <- lm(formula = Yield ~ Fertilizer + Rainfall, data = df4) summary(fm4) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Call: lm(formula = Yield ~ Fertilizer + Rainfall, data = df4) Residuals: 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 -0.2381 -2.3810 2.1429 1.6667 1.1905 -2.6190 0.2381 Coefficients: Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|) (Intercept) 28.095238 2.491482 11.277 0.000352 *** Fertilizer 0.038095 0.005832 6.532 0.002838 ** Rainfall 0.833333 0.154303 5.401 0.005690 ** --- Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 Residual standard error: 2.315 on 4 degrees of freedom Multiple R-squared: 0.9814, Adjusted R-squared: 0.972 F-statistic: 105.3 on 2 and 4 DF, p-value: 0.0003472 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} anova(fm4) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Analysis of Variance Table Response: Yield Df Sum Sq Mean Sq F value Pr(>F) Fertilizer 1 972.32 972.32 181.500 0.0001756 *** Rainfall 1 156.25 156.25 29.167 0.0056899 ** Residuals 4 21.43 5.36 --- Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} library(lm.beta) # if (!require("lm.beta")) install.packages("lm.beta") lm.beta(fm4) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Call: lm(formula = Yield ~ Fertilizer + Rainfall, data = df4) Standardized Coefficients:: (Intercept) Fertilizer Rainfall NA 0.5944301 0.4914732 ``` ::: :::   ## Polynomial Regression Analysis > * Quantifying non-linear dependency of a normal response on quantitative explanatory variable(s) ### Example > An experiment was conducted to evaluate the effects of different levels of nitrogen. Three levels of nitrogen: 0, 10 and 20 grams per plot were used in the experiment. Each treatment was replicated twice and data is given below: Nitrogen | Yield ------------| ------------- 0 | 5 0 | 7 10 | 15 10 | 17 20 | 9 20 | 11 ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} Nitrogen <- c(0, 0, 10, 10, 20, 20) Yield <- c(5, 7, 15, 17, 9, 11) df5 <- data.frame(Nitrogen, Yield) df5 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Nitrogen Yield 1 0 5 2 0 7 3 10 15 4 10 17 5 20 9 6 20 11 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} library(ggplot2) # if (!require("ggplot2")) install.packages("ggplot2") p5 <- ggplot(data = df5, mapping = aes(x = Nitrogen, y = Yield)) + geom_point() + labs(x = "Nitrogen", y = "Yield") + theme_bw() print(p5) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](2020-03-06_UAF_files/figure-html/reg.p5-1.svg){fig-align='center' width=672} ::: ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} fm5 <- lm(formula = Yield ~ Nitrogen + I(Nitrogen^2), data = df5) # fm5 <- lm(formula = Yield ~ poly(x = Nitrogen, degree = 2, raw = TRUE), data = df5) summary(fm5) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Call: lm(formula = Yield ~ Nitrogen + I(Nitrogen^2), data = df5) Residuals: 1 2 3 4 5 6 -1 1 -1 1 -1 1 Coefficients: Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|) (Intercept) 6.00000 1.00000 6.000 0.00927 ** Nitrogen 1.80000 0.25495 7.060 0.00584 ** I(Nitrogen^2) -0.08000 0.01225 -6.532 0.00729 ** --- Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 Residual standard error: 1.414 on 3 degrees of freedom Multiple R-squared: 0.9441, Adjusted R-squared: 0.9068 F-statistic: 25.33 on 2 and 3 DF, p-value: 0.01322 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} anova(fm5) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Analysis of Variance Table Response: Yield Df Sum Sq Mean Sq F value Pr(>F) Nitrogen 1 16.000 16.000 8.000 0.066276 . I(Nitrogen^2) 1 85.333 85.333 42.667 0.007292 ** Residuals 3 6.000 2.000 --- Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 ``` ::: :::   ## Analysis of Variance (ANOVA) > * Comparing means of Normal dependent variable for levels of different factor(s)   ::: {#fig-ANOVANormalMeans} ![](./fig/ANOVANormalMeans.webp) Analysis of Variance :::   ### Example Yield | Variety ------------| ------------- 5 | V1 6 | V1 7 | V1 15 | V2 16 | V2 17 | V2 ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} Yield <- c(5, 6, 7, 15, 16, 17) Variety <- gl(n = 2, k = 3, length = 2*3, labels = c("V1", "V2")) df6 <- data.frame(Yield, Variety) df6 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Yield Variety 1 5 V1 2 6 V1 3 7 V1 4 15 V2 5 16 V2 6 17 V2 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} library(ggplot2) # if (!require("ggplot2")) install.packages("ggplot2") p6 <- ggplot(data = df6, mapping = aes(x = Variety, y = Yield)) + geom_point() + geom_boxplot() + labs(x = "Variety", y = "Yield") + theme_bw() print(p6) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](2020-03-06_UAF_files/figure-html/anova.p6-1.svg){fig-align='center' width=672} ::: ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} fm6 <- lm(formula = Yield ~ Variety, data = df6) summary(fm6) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Call: lm(formula = Yield ~ Variety, data = df6) Residuals: 1 2 3 4 5 6 -1.000e+00 -4.774e-15 1.000e+00 -1.000e+00 -1.110e-16 1.000e+00 Coefficients: Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|) (Intercept) 6.0000 0.5774 10.39 0.000484 *** VarietyV2 10.0000 0.8165 12.25 0.000255 *** --- Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 Residual standard error: 1 on 4 degrees of freedom Multiple R-squared: 0.974, Adjusted R-squared: 0.9675 F-statistic: 150 on 1 and 4 DF, p-value: 0.0002552 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} anova(fm6) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Analysis of Variance Table Response: Yield Df Sum Sq Mean Sq F value Pr(>F) Variety 1 150 150 150 0.0002552 *** Residuals 4 4 1 --- Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 ``` ::: :::   ### Example Yield | Variety ------------| ------------- 5 | V1 7 | V1 15 | V2 17 | V2 17 | V3 19 | V3 ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} Yield <- c(5, 7, 15, 17, 17, 19) Variety <- gl(n = 3, k = 2, length = 3*2, labels = c("V1", "V2", "V3")) df7 <- data.frame(Yield, Variety) df7 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Yield Variety 1 5 V1 2 7 V1 3 15 V2 4 17 V2 5 17 V3 6 19 V3 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} library(ggplot2) # if (!require("ggplot2")) install.packages("ggplot2") p7 <- ggplot(data = df7, mapping = aes(x = Variety, y = Yield)) + geom_point() + geom_boxplot() + labs(x = "Variety", y = "Yield") + theme_bw() print(p7) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](2020-03-06_UAF_files/figure-html/anova.p7-1.svg){fig-align='center' width=672} ::: ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} fm7 <- lm(formula = Yield ~ Variety, data = df7) summary(fm7) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Call: lm(formula = Yield ~ Variety, data = df7) Residuals: 1 2 3 4 5 6 -1 1 -1 1 -1 1 Coefficients: Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|) (Intercept) 6.000 1.000 6.000 0.00927 ** VarietyV2 10.000 1.414 7.071 0.00582 ** VarietyV3 12.000 1.414 8.485 0.00344 ** --- Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 Residual standard error: 1.414 on 3 degrees of freedom Multiple R-squared: 0.965, Adjusted R-squared: 0.9416 F-statistic: 41.33 on 2 and 3 DF, p-value: 0.006553 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} anova(fm7) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Analysis of Variance Table Response: Yield Df Sum Sq Mean Sq F value Pr(>F) Variety 2 165.33 82.667 41.333 0.006553 ** Residuals 3 6.00 2.000 --- Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 ``` ::: :::   ## Analysis of Covariance (ANCOVA) > * Quantifying dependency of a normal response on quantitative explanatory variable(s) * Comparing means of Normal dependent variable for levels of different factor(s)   ::: {#fig-ANCOVA} ![](./fig/ANCOVA.webp) Analysis of Covariance :::   ### Example Yield | Fert | Variety ------------| -------|----------- 51 | 80 | V1 52 | 80 | V1 53 | 90 | V1 54 | 90 | V1 56 | 100 | V1 57 | 100 | V1 55 | 80 | V2 56 | 80 | V2 58 | 90 | V2 59 | 90 | V2 62 | 100 | V2 63 | 100 | V2   ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} Yield <- c(51, 52, 53, 54, 56, 57, 55, 56, 58, 59, 62, 63) Fert <- rep(x = c(80, 90, 100), each = 2) Variety <- gl(n = 2, k = 6, length = 2*6, labels = c("V1", "V2"), ordered = FALSE) df8 <- data.frame(Yield, Fert, Variety) df8 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Yield Fert Variety 1 51 80 V1 2 52 80 V1 3 53 90 V1 4 54 90 V1 5 56 100 V1 6 57 100 V1 7 55 80 V2 8 56 80 V2 9 58 90 V2 10 59 90 V2 11 62 100 V2 12 63 100 V2 ``` ::: ::: ### Same intercepts but different slopes ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} fm8 <- lm(formula = Yield ~ Fert + Variety, data = df8) summary(fm8) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Call: lm(formula = Yield ~ Fert + Variety, data = df8) Residuals: Min 1Q Median 3Q Max -0.8333 -0.8333 0.1667 0.1667 1.1667 Coefficients: Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|) (Intercept) 26.83333 2.54557 10.54 2.30e-06 *** Fert 0.30000 0.02805 10.69 2.04e-06 *** VarietyV2 5.00000 0.45812 10.91 1.72e-06 *** --- Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 Residual standard error: 0.7935 on 9 degrees of freedom Multiple R-squared: 0.9629, Adjusted R-squared: 0.9546 F-statistic: 116.7 on 2 and 9 DF, p-value: 3.657e-07 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} anova(fm8) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Analysis of Variance Table Response: Yield Df Sum Sq Mean Sq F value Pr(>F) Fert 1 72.000 72.00 114.35 2.042e-06 *** Variety 1 75.000 75.00 119.12 1.720e-06 *** Residuals 9 5.667 0.63 --- Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} library(ggplot2) # if (!require("ggplot2")) install.packages("ggplot2") p8 <- ggplot(data = df8, mapping = aes(x = Fert, y = Yield, color = Variety)) + geom_point() + geom_line(mapping = aes(y = predict(fm8))) + labs(x = "Fert", y = "Yield") + theme_bw() print(p8) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](2020-03-06_UAF_files/figure-html/ancova.p8-1.svg){fig-align='center' width=672} ::: :::   ### Different intercepts and different slopes ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} fm9 <- lm(formula = Yield ~ Fert * Variety, data = df8) summary(fm9) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Call: lm(formula = Yield ~ Fert * Variety, data = df8) Residuals: Min 1Q Median 3Q Max -0.83333 -0.33333 -0.08333 0.66667 0.66667 Coefficients: Estimate Std. Completely Randomized Design (CRD) > * Used when experimental material is homogeneous   ### Example > The following table shows some of the results of an experiment on the effects of applications of sulphur in reducing scab disease of potatoes. The object in applying sulphur is to increase the acidity of the soil, since scab does not thrive in very acid soil. In addition to untreated plots which serve as a control, 3 amounts of dressing were compared—300, 600, and 900 lb. per acre. Both a fall and a spring application of each amount was tested, so that in all there were seven distinct treatments. The sulphur was spread by hand on the surface of the soil, and then diced into a depth of about 4 inches. The quantity to be analyzed is the “scab index”. That is roughly speaking, the percentage of the surface area of the potato that is infected with scab. It is obtained by examining 100 potatoes at random from each plot, grading each potato on a scale from 0 to 100% infected, and taking the average. ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} ScabIndex <- c( 12, 10, 24, 29, 30, 18, 32, 26, 9, 9, 16, 4, 30, 7, 21, 9, 16, 10, 18, 18, 18, 24, 12, 19, 10, 4, 4, 5, 17, 7, 16, 17 ) Trt <- factor( x = rep(x = 1:7, times = c(8, 4, 4, 4, 4, 4, 4)) , labels = c("O", "F3", "S3", "F6", "S6", "F9", "S9") ) df13 <- data.frame(Trt, ScabIndex) df13 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Trt ScabIndex 1 O 12 2 O 10 3 O 24 4 O 29 5 O 30 6 O 18 7 O 32 8 O 26 9 F3 9 10 F3 9 11 F3 16 12 F3 4 13 S3 30 14 S3 7 15 S3 21 16 S3 9 17 F6 16 18 F6 10 19 F6 18 20 F6 18 21 S6 18 22 S6 24 23 S6 12 24 S6 19 25 F9 10 26 F9 4 27 F9 4 28 F9 5 29 S9 17 30 S9 7 31 S9 16 32 S9 17 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} library(ggplot2) # if (!require("ggplot2")) install.packages("ggplot2") p13 <- ggplot(data = df13, mapping = aes(x = Trt, y = ScabIndex)) + geom_point() + geom_boxplot() + labs(x = "Treatment", y = "Scab Index") + theme_bw() print(p13) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](2020-03-06_UAF_files/figure-html/crd.p13-1.svg){fig-align='center' width=672} ::: ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} fm13 <- lm(formula = ScabIndex ~ Trt, data = df13) summary(fm13) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Call: lm(formula = ScabIndex ~ Trt, data = df13) Residuals: Min 1Q Median 3Q Max -12.625 -4.844 0.625 3.594 13.250 Coefficients: Estimate Std.
